data_coverage_summary: The Battery Passport REST API provides standardized access to battery passport records and related organization, connector, analytics, and schema information used to manage battery passport data exchange across the ecosystem.
buyer_guidance: Use this integration when the customer needs governed battery passport records and related organizational context in ServiceNow. It is strongest for registry-style product, compliance, and ecosystem exchange data rather than live telemetry streaming.
overview: The Battery Passport API is a business-data API rather than a telemetry feed. Based on the Siemens API reference, it exposes CRUD and lookup access across passport records, passport hierarchy, schema definitions, attribute assignments, connector configuration, organization data, user roles, and export-oriented flows. That makes it suitable for onboarding structured passport and ecosystem data into ServiceNow CMDB-adjacent, supplier, compliance, or sustainability workflows.

available_data.description: Core passport objects and their lifecycle state, including public and private retrieval patterns.
data_points: public passport data for external exchange
private passport data for authenticated users
ancestor, child, and descendant passport relationships

available_data.description: Open assignment records used to request, update, remind, and close data obligations tied to passports or collaborating organizations.
Identity and access context for the organizations participating in the battery passport ecosystem.
Configuration of partner-facing connectors used to expose or exchange passport data with external ecosystems such as Catena-X.
data_points: passport exposure actions to connector endpoints

available_data.description: Administrative structures that support reporting, feature enablement, and schema-driven validation of passport content.

ingest_considerations: Treat this as structured master and exchange data rather than high-frequency operational telemetry.
Public and private passport retrieval paths imply data segmentation and entitlement handling will matter in the ServiceNow integration design.
Connector endpoints and organization-user data may be as commercially useful as the passport records themselves for governance workflows.
Schema-definition endpoints suggest payload evolution can be managed explicitly and should be captured in implementation planning.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: This proof-of-concept summary is based on the Siemens API reference groupings visible on the Battery Passport documentation page, including Passport Management, Attribute Assignments, Organizations, Users, Connectors, Feature Config, Analytics, and Data Schema Management.
